title: Severity vs. Priority: How to Classify a Bug Correctly
excerpt: Severity is how badly a bug breaks the product. Priority is how soon you should fix it. They're independent — and confusing them is why the wrong bugs get worked first. Here's how to classify each correctly.

title: How to fix flaky end-to-end tests (and stop re-running CI)
excerpt: Flaky tests are a symptom, not bad luck. Here are the five root causes — timing, shared state, network races, test order, and retry abuse — and how to fix each one for good.

title: How to reduce 'cannot reproduce' bug tickets for good
excerpt: 'Cannot reproduce' is the most expensive two words in a bug tracker. Here's why it happens — and the four kinds of state you have to capture to make it stop.

title: How to write a bug report developers will actually act on
excerpt: A bug report gets fixed fast when it carries three things: exact steps, the environment, and evidence (screenshot + console + network). Here's the format.

title: Capturing Network Requests for Bug Reports That Get Fixed
excerpt: Console logs show errors, but network requests show why they happen. Learn which requests to capture, what fields matter, and how to include them in a bug report developers can act on immediately.

title: Playwright vs. Cypress: Choosing Your Regression Test Framework
excerpt: Both Playwright and Cypress catch regressions — but they make different trade-offs around speed, flakiness, cross-browser coverage, and CI cost. Here's how to pick the right one for your team.

title: Why Your QA Team Finds Different Bugs Than Your Real Users
excerpt: QA teams are expert users who know what to avoid. Real users aren't — and the bugs they hit live in the paths your QA team never takes. Here's how to close that gap systematically.

title: Browser Console Errors: Signal vs. Noise in QA Triage
excerpt: Not every console error is a bug. Not every bug announces itself as an error. Here's how to read console output in under two minutes and separate what matters from what doesn't.

title: How AI Personas Catch Bugs Your Test Suite Misses
excerpt: Automated test suites verify what you thought to test. AI personas explore what you didn't think to test — surfacing UX bugs, edge-case interactions, and assumption mismatches before real users find them.

title: How to Triage a Bug Backlog Without Losing Your Mind
excerpt: A 200-bug backlog doesn't mean 200 bugs to fix — it means 200 items to sort so the five that actually matter rise to the top. Here's a repeatable triage process that keeps your backlog useful.

title: What Are Self-Healing End-to-End Tests? A Practical Guide
excerpt: Self-healing tests auto-repair broken selectors when your UI changes, so a renamed button doesn't fail the whole suite. Here's how they actually work.

title: How to File a Bug to Jira, Linear, or GitHub From the Browser
excerpt: File a bug to your tracker without losing context: capture evidence first, map it to each tool's required fields, and search before filing to avoid dupes.

title: Session Replay vs. Bug Reports: When to Use Each Tool
excerpt: Session replay records everything and hopes you find the bug later. Bug reports capture the exact moment a person flags one. Which should you reach for?

title: What Is Synthetic User Testing? A Practical Definition
excerpt: Synthetic user testing uses AI personas built from real customer data to simulate how users navigate your product and surface usability bugs early.

title: Jira vs. Linear vs. GitHub Issues: Which Bug Tracker?
excerpt: Jira, Linear, and GitHub Issues all track bugs, but they optimize for different teams. Here's how they compare on setup, workflows, cost, and code proximity.

title: How to Turn Customer Interview Transcripts Into Personas
excerpt: Customer interview transcripts are full of persona signal: goals, blockers, and the words users use. Here's how to turn them into testable personas.

title: How to Deduplicate Bug Reports Before Your Backlog Drowns
excerpt: Duplicate bug reports waste triage time and split the signal across tickets. Here's how to match, merge, and prevent duplicates before your backlog drowns.

title: How to Turn Voice-of-Customer Data Into Product Decisions
excerpt: Voice-of-customer data only helps if it changes what you build. Here's how to code raw feedback, weight it by impact, and ship the decision.

title: Exploratory Testing vs. Scripted Testing: When to Use Each
excerpt: Exploratory testing discovers the unknown; scripted testing verifies the known. Here's what each catches, when to use which, and how to combine them.

title: What Is AI Bug Reporting? Definition and How It Works
excerpt: AI bug reporting uses AI to turn a rough report into a complete, reproducible ticket — capturing steps, environment, and evidence automatically.

title: Manual QA vs. Automated QA: How to Split the Work Right
excerpt: Manual and automated QA catch different bugs at different costs. Automate the stable, repetitive checks and keep humans on judgment. Here's the split.

title: In-App Bug Reporting: A Practical Guide for QA Teams
excerpt: In-app bug reporting lets people file a bug from inside your product, so it ships with the exact state — URL, console, network, and environment — attached.

title: How to Run a Bug Bash That Actually Finds Real Bugs
excerpt: A bug bash only works when it's scoped, timeboxed, and captures reproducible evidence. Here's how to plan one, run the session, and triage what it finds.

title: The QA Metrics That Actually Matter (and Which to Drop)
excerpt: Test pass rate and bug count look busy but hide quality. Track escaped defects, reopen rate, MTTR, and flakiness instead. Here's why, and how.
